evals.prompt: I need a pricing page for a SaaS product with three tiers: Free, Pro ($29/mo), and Enterprise (custom pricing). Each tier has 4-5 feature bullet points. Design it from scratch as a complete HTML page using good design principles — typography, spacing, hierarchy, color. The product is a developer tool.
evals.expected_output: A complete HTML page with a well-structured pricing section. Should demonstrate: clear visual hierarchy between tiers, consistent spacing system, restrained color palette, strong typographic scale, highlighted/recommended tier.

evals.prompt: Please audit the design of this landing page and give me specific, actionable feedback. Here's the layout: a full-width hero section with a centered headline ('The fastest way to ship'), a subtitle, and a blue CTA button. Below that, a section with the heading 'Features' and 6 cards in a 2-column grid, each with an icon, title, and 2 sentences of description. The cards have a light grey background, a drop shadow, and a 2px blue border on the left side. Then a testimonials section with 3 quotes side by side, each in a box with a yellow background. The font is Arial throughout at the same size for everything.
evals.expected_output: A structured design audit using the review format: overall impression, what's working, specific issues with problem/why/fix, and priority fixes. Should catch: no typographic hierarchy (same size for everything), overuse of decorative elements (shadows + borders + yellow backgrounds), color inconsistency, lack of spacing system.

evals.prompt: Create a clean, professional one-page project brief template as an HTML file. It should have sections for: project title, one-line summary, background/context, goals (as a list), scope (in/out of scope), timeline, and team. Design it so it looks like something from a top design studio — minimal, typographic, Swiss aesthetic.
evals.expected_output: An HTML file that looks like a professionally designed document. Should demonstrate: strong typographic hierarchy, consistent spacing, restrained color use (mostly black/white with one accent), clear section structure, grid-based layout.
